items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes (CVE-2026-53787) allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento (PHP object injection)
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a full-page cache extension for Magento and Adobe Commerce, allowing remote code execution. The flaw is tracked as CVE-2026-45247 and rated 9.8 (critical).

items.title: Stored XSS via Server-Side Template Injection (APSB25-50 related)
items.summary: Adobe Commerce versions are affected by a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability via server-side template injection, which was addressed in a security update (APSB25-50) and for which an isolated patch for CVE-2025-47110 was released.

items.title: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source Security Update
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store using a polyglot to bypass validation. Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 containing the fix as part of APSB25-94.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update (APSB26-05) for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source to address multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.
